body.title: [sprint] Convert ~40 archive subsystem/design docs into architecture/ intention-points
body.description:
**Problem:** `~/TheIgors.archive/lab/design_docs/` holds ~40 subsystem/architecture .md docs (announce-protocol-design, datacenter-swarm-bus-design, subsystem_cognition, etc.) that describe how subsystems were intended to work. CLAUDE.md's canonical form for these is a uniform `architecture/<subsystem>.json` intention-point (intent summary + pointers to implementing files). UU architecture/ has only 9 today.
**Proposed shape:** Triage the ~40 docs: for each subsystem still live in UU, write/extend an `architecture/<subsystem>.json` intention-point capturing the intent and pointing at the CURRENT implementing files (verify the files still exist post-single-package-reorg — paths are now under unseen_university/devices/). For docs describing retired subsystems, capture as historical architecture notes or skip with a logged reason. This is judgment-heavy: a hollow copy is not the deliverable — each intent-point must point at real current code or be marked historical.
**Affected files:** writes to devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/; reads ~/TheIgors.archive/lab/design_docs/*.md. No code changes.
**Design rules:** architecture intention-points are JSON (intent -> implementation), one-home store; verify implementation paths against the post-reorg tree (unseen_university/devices/...); CP1 (mark historical honestly rather than fabricate a live pointer).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the ~40 subsystem/design .md in archive lab/design_docs/ (excluding the decisions/ subdir). OUT — the 116 decisions (separate ticket); .dsb palace files (freeze); docs that duplicate an existing architecture/ intent-point (extend, don't duplicate).
**Test plan:** (1) Each in-scope doc results in either a new/extended architecture/<subsystem>.json OR a logged skip-reason. (2) For 5 sampled live intent-points, the file pointers resolve to real paths in the current tree. (3) `ls devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/ | wc -l` rises. (4) No intent-point points at a pre-reorg path (devices/ or config/ at repo root).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
